gloss:
The two gaps in the causal hierarchy have different sources. The association-to-intervention gap comes from interventions: two models indistinguishable by observation separate once interventions are applied. The intervention-to-counterfactual gap comes from observables: the probability-of-necessity-and-sufficiency quantity is unreadable even at the full intervention quotient, yet readable from response-type observables with no interventions.</corrected_gloss>
</invoke>


**Cites** `WMSpec.Transform.cz_omega_typed_gaps` — the corpus theorem this result restates and discharges.

**Original contribution.** The raw lemma bundles several facts; the thesis-layer statement diagnoses the two hierarchy gaps as living on two orthogonal axes (interventions vs observables) of one connection, one gap on each.

statement.pretty: (∃ M M', (WMSpec.Transform.wordBisim (WMSpec.Transform.obsWords Bool) (WMSpec.Transform.bowFam Bool)) M M' ∧ ¬(WMSpec.Transform.wordBisim (WMSpec.Transform.intWords Bool) (WMSpec.Transform.bowFam Bool)) M M') ∧ WMSpec.Transform.BowSCM.pnsRead ∉ WMSpec.Transform.readable (WMSpec.Transform.wordBisim (WMSpec.Transform.surgWords (Bool × Bool)) (WMSpec.Transform.bowFam (Bool × Bool))) ∧ WMSpec.Transform.BowSCM.pnsRead ∈ WMSpec.Transform.readable (WMSpec.Transform.wordBisim {id} (WMSpec.Transform.rtFam (Bool × Bool)))

title: Eidometry.Thesis.causal_typed_gaps
